title: HARM REDUCTION AND SYRINGE SERVICES - 211info
text: TILLAMOOK COUNTY COMMUNITY HEALTH CENTERS PUBLIC HEALTH HARM REDUCTION AND SYRINGE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides new needles and a safe sharps disposal service. Offers hygiene and harm reduction supplies, including alcohol swabs, condoms, wound care items, and Naloxone/Narcan training and kits. Connects individuals with community resources and recovery support. All services are anonymous. 2111 8th Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Tillamook 2nd and 4th Wednesday of the month 11am-1pm The Herald Center 2710 First Street Tillamook, OR 97141, -Pacific City 1st Thursday of the month 11am-1pm Brooten Road Pacific City, OR 97135 -Wheeler 3rd Thursday of the month 11am-1pm Parking lot above North County Food Bank 278 Rowe St, Wheeler, OR 97147 Email (503) 842-3943 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 842-3943 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in. $20.00 Fred Meyer gift card incentive for qualifying partcipants, limit one card per month/per participant. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Tillamook County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 7am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located at 8th Street and Pacific Avenue Services: Contraception Needle Exchange and Injection Supplies Opioid Antidote Distribution Sharps Disposal Programs Specialized Information and Referral * Substance Use Disorder Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER REGULATION - FOOD HANDLER CARD ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AND IMPROVEMENT - WATER QUALITY ASSURANCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BABIES FIRST! INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CACOON PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM PUBLIC HEALTH - PUBLIC HEALTH FACILITY HEALTH INSPECTIONS SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - NALOXBOX If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
